phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your worked-ground boulder bursts beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — worked-ground boulder bursts edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your worked-ground boulder bursts beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — worked-ground boulder bursts edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your worked-ground boulder bursts on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your worked-ground boulder bursts in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Anivia's wall-frost poke control own this window — your worked-ground boulder bursts don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q on fresh ground shreds level-1 trades — most mids don't expect five stones at minute two. Manage your Worked Ground like mana.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the shove-displacement already threatens kill angles against overstep-happy laners.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: W-into-E is the signature burst — shove him across the stones and watch half a health bar disappear.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your worked-ground boulder bursts out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your worked-ground boulder bursts beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's spike: the W-E-Q rotation deletes squishies. Force river fights where your stones pre-seed the geometry.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Taliyah edits maps: wall their reinforcements, shove their frontline into your team, and Q whatever stands on stones.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Weaver's Wall online — you are now the best roaming mid in the game. Every shove is a bot-lane gank announcement.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's spike: the W-E-Q rotation deletes squishies. Force river fights where your stones pre-seed the geometry.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Taliyah edits maps: wall their reinforcements, shove their frontline into your team, and Q whatever stands on stones.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q on fresh ground shreds level-1 trades — most mids don't expect five stones at minute two. Manage your Worked Ground like mana.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the shove-displacement already threatens kill angles against overstep-happy laners.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: W-into-E is the signature burst — shove him across the stones and watch half a health bar disappear.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — Q-poke on every wave contact. Fresh-ground positioning is your real skill expression now.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Weaver's Wall online — you are now the best roaming mid in the game. Every shove is a bot-lane gank announcement.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving own this window — your worked-ground boulder bursts don't answer them on first item yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.
